KEY SHIFT THIS TICK — concentration trajectory is now IMPROVING. BFSI % of deployed book: 61.7% at 13:00 → 52.6% at 13:30 (-9.1 pp), because the three new fills this window were a mixed basket: AUBANK (BFSI, +5.25%) paired with NESTLEIND (FMCG, +4.94%) and GRANULES (pharma, +4.77%) — two of three non-BFSI diversifiers. SINGLE-NAME RED FLAG — AUBANK aggregate exposure: filled 50 sh (5.25%) + OPEN 70 sh (7.35%) + OPEN 56 sh (5.86%) = 176 sh × ~₹1,049 = ₹1,84,632 on full fill = 18.46% of capital. This BREACHES the 15% max_position_pct cap set at 12:30. Mechanism: the two OPEN AUBANK lots (12:08, 12:39) were accepted when the cap was 0.20 or when total AUBANK was zero; aggregate-post-fill check was not applied. The breach is structural from pre-cap orders in flight — no new AUBANK add has occurred since the 12:30 cap was set. The cap itself will block any *further* AUBANK entry now (aggregate is already > cap), so self-limiting. Worth flagging but does not require a new lever this tick.

actions_taken: NO NEW TIGHTENING this tick. Rationale: two levers were pulled in the prior 60 minutes (max_position_pct 0.20→0.15 at 12:30, min_confidence 0.55→0.60 at 13:00) and the 13:00→13:30 window shows them working — three new fills but with a 2:1 non-BFSI:BFSI mix (NESTLEIND + GRANULES vs AUBANK), BFSI share of deployed down 9 pp, no new OPEN orders accumulated. Pulling a third lever now would be reactive rather than evidence-driven.

Retained max_position_pct = 0.15 (set 12:30). AUBANK aggregate on full fill = 18.46% breaches this cap, but the breach is STRUCTURAL from two OPEN orders accepted before the cap was effective (12:08 and 12:39 lots), not from a post-cap violation. The cap itself will block any NEW AUBANK entry now (since aggregate already > cap), so the risk is self-limiting. Tightening to 0.12 would not force the in-flight OPEN orders to cancel and would over-constrain future diversifier entries that are currently desirable.

Retained min_confidence = 0.60 (set 13:00). The 3 fills in the 13:09–13:11 window all cleared 0.60. That they filled is not evidence the lever is ineffective — it is evidence the lever allows qualifying entries while blocking sub-0.60 conviction. No new OPEN orders after 12:39 is the behavioural signal that qualification pressure increased.

Did NOT add max_deployed_pct override (base 0.80). Projected deployment on full fill is 63.84% — inside base cap with ~16 pp headroom. Adding a 0.70 override now would be preemptive and risks clipping pending fills if any OPEN order prices drift up on execution.
Did NOT tighten daily_loss_limit_pct (0.03) or drawdown thresholds (10% / 15%). Realized P&L is ₹0 and no drawdown has materialized. Tightening loss gates with zero losses is noise.

Did NOT tighten default_sl_pct (0.02), min_sl_pct (0.005), max_sl_pct (0.05), or min_risk_reward (1.5). No evidence that exit mechanics or R:R quality are the risk vector — the vector is concentration, which the two existing overrides target at sizing and qualification layers.

Flagged for next tick (14:00): monitor whether AUBANK aggregate (18.46% projected) materially trips any downstream sizing or exit logic once the OPEN orders fill. If the BFSI % of deployed begins re-inflecting UP, or if any new BFSI entry is attempted and admitted, the next lever candidate is max_position_pct 0.15→0.12 (targets single-name stacking directly).
